message:
fix(demo-showcase): add warmup to inspector stress budget tests (bd-274xl)

The first render iteration incurs cold-start overhead (page faults,
cache fills) that can inflate the average past tight budget limits.
Added a single warmup iteration before timing starts in all 6 budget-
checked inspector stress tests to eliminate variance.

Previously stress_overlay_render_widget_bounds_many_widgets was flaky
(avg 5.4ms vs 5.0ms budget). With warmup it consistently passes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-130l.5","title":"Responsive Demo and Testing","description":"Demo showcase and test infrastructure for responsive features.

## Requirements
- Demo screen with responsive layout
- Visual breakpoint indicator
- Snapshot tests at each breakpoint
- E2E resize simulation

## Implementation
- Responsive demo in ftui-demo-showcase
- Breakpoint badge widget
- Parameterized snapshot tests

## Success Criteria
- Demo clearly shows breakpoint changes
- All breakpoints covered in tests
- No layout glitches on resize

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Responsive Demo and Testing
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-130l.6","title":"Responsive Breakpoints E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of responsive layout behavior across breakpoints.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Breakpoint Transitions (responsive_breakpoint_e2e.rs)
- Resize through all breakpoints
- Style changes at boundaries
- Layout changes at boundaries
- No flicker during transition

### 2. Style Variants (responsive_style_e2e.rs)
- Verify style at each breakpoint
- Inheritance from smaller breakpoints
- Override specificity

### 3. Layout Switching (responsive_layout_e2e.rs)
- Grid to stack at small sizes
- Column count changes
- Widget visibility changes
- State preservation across switches

### 4. Edge Cases (responsive_edge_e2e.rs)
- Rapid resize events
- Resize during animation
- Extreme aspect ratios
- Minimum viable size

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Breakpoint changes with old/new
- DEBUG: Layout recalculation triggers
- TRACE: Style resolution chain
- Metric: Resize response latency

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/responsive/

## Snapshot Matrix
- Test at 40, 80, 120, 160 columns
- Test at 10, 24, 40 rows
- Cross-product all combinations

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Responsive Breakpoints E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-13gzg","title":"Close GH #10: docs substantially addressed, note crates.io gap","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #10 filed by schpet (2026-02-06). Requested documentation for library consumers.

## Current State: SUBSTANTIALLY ADDRESSED
Significant documentation work was done the same day:
- docs/getting-started.md: Comprehensive guide with stability notes, crate map, prerequisites, copy-paste minimal app (~75 lines), common patterns, troubleshooting, example index
- docs/tutorials/agent-harness.md: 12 KB agent-harness tutorial
- Commit 92d4ba5d: "fix docs API drift + add CI doc/example gates"
- 5 working examples at crates/ftui-harness/examples/ (minimal, counter, layout, modal, streaming)
- ftui facade crate (crates/ftui/src/lib.rs) provides clean public API surface

## Remaining Gap
Getting-started guide recommends path dependencies (path = "../frankentui/crates/ftui") because most crates aren't on crates.io. Only ftui-core, ftui-layout, and ftui-i18n are published. The ftui facade and its transitive deps need publishing.

## Action
1. Close GH #10 with comment pointing to docs/getting-started.md and docs/tutorials/agent-harness.md
2. Note that crates.io publishing of ftui facade is the remaining gap for wider adoption
3. Note CI is also failing, which undermines confidence for library consumers

## Also note
The frankentui CI is failing (all 5 recent runs show failure). This is a separate issue but worth tracking.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:20:23.374644405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:28:17.898516608Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:28:17.898484999Z","close_reason":"Closed GH #10 with summary comment pointing to docs/getting-started.md and noting crates.io publishing gap","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/frankentui#10","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crates-io","docs","housekeeping"]}

 {"id":"bd-13pq","title":"Demo Showcase: Async Task Manager / Job Queue","description":"## Background

Modern TUIs are reactive systems: background work, progress updates, cancellations, and rate limits.

This screen demonstrates a job/task manager with:
- concurrent tasks with progress
- cancellation
- scheduling policies that are explainable and testable

The point is not "toy concurrency"; it is to prove the runtime + UI can represent and control a realistic workload.

## UX / Core Interactions

- A table/list of jobs with:
  - name
  - state (queued/running/succeeded/failed/canceled)
  - progress
  - runtime
  - priority
- Controls:
  - spawn job
  - cancel selected
  - cancel all
  - change scheduler mode

Suggested keybindings:
- N: spawn a new job
- C: cancel selected job
- S: cycle scheduler policy
- R: retry failed job

## Architecture Notes

- Determinism is mandatory for tests:
  - jobs should be driven by a deterministic clock/stepper in test mode
  - avoid wall-clock coupling in core scheduling logic

- Scheduling policies (bd-13pq.7):
  - implement as pure functions over job state + metrics
  - explicitly define objective (latency, fairness, throughput)
  - stable tie-breaking

- Cancellation (bd-13pq.8):
  - model cancellation as a state transition with audit events
  - ensure no resource leaks

## Acceptance Criteria

- Jobs progress and complete with visible updates.
- Cancellation is immediate and leaves the system consistent.
- Scheduler policies are user-visible and explainable (why this job ran next).

Additionally (project-wide standards):
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

## Alien Artifact Upgrades (Concrete)

- Queueing theory scheduler (bd-13pq.7):
  - define a small model (e.g., weighted fair scheduling vs shortest-remaining-time)
  - provide a comparison harness with deterministic traces
- Hazard-based cancellation (bd-13pq.8):
  - treat "should we cancel?" as expected-loss minimization with explicit costs
  - document assumptions and emit an evidence ledger entry when a policy triggers

## Extreme Optimization

- Budget the per-tick update overhead.
- Provide a stress test with many jobs and verify the UI remains responsive.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:12.257537916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:47:53.338737861Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T17:47:53.338713265Z","close_reason":"Full implementation complete: 6 scheduling policies with queueing theory foundations, hazard-based cancellation, aging fairness, diagnostic logging with JSONL, 91 unit tests passing, 17 snapshot tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","ux"],"comments":[{"id":295,"issue_id":"bd-13pq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scope
- Spawn multiple concurrent jobs with progress + cancel.
- Show task states and a summary dashboard.

Testing
- Unit tests for task state transitions and cancellation.
- PTY E2E tests with verbose logs for job lifecycle.

 {"id":"bd-27kx","title":"Text Effects: Organic Breathing Pulse","description":"## Background
Simple sine wave pulsing looks mechanical. Organic breathing has asymmetric timing (quick inhale, slow exhale) and per-character variation for a living, natural feel.

## Implementation

Add TextEffect variant:
```rust
TextEffect::OrganicPulse {
    speed: f64,            // Breaths per second (0.5 = one breath every 2s)
    min_brightness: f64,   // 0.0-1.0, lowest brightness
    asymmetry: f64,        // 0.0=symmetric, 1.0=very asymmetric (quick in, slow out)
    phase_variation: f64,  // 0.0-1.0, how much chars vary in phase
    seed: u64,             // Deterministic randomness for phase offsets
}
```

### Asymmetric Breathing Curve:
```rust
fn breathing_curve(t: f64, asymmetry: f64) -> f64 {
    // t is 0-1 through one breath cycle
    // asymmetry controls rise/fall ratio
    let rise_duration = 0.3 - asymmetry * 0.2;  // 10-30% of cycle for inhale
    
    if t < rise_duration {
        // Quick rise (inhale) - use quadratic ease-out
        let local_t = t / rise_duration;
        1.0 - (1.0 - local_t) * (1.0 - local_t)  // Ease-out
    } else {
        // Slow fall (exhale) - use quadratic ease-in-out
        let local_t = (t - rise_duration) / (1.0 - rise_duration);
        if local_t < 0.5 {
            1.0 - 2.0 * local_t * local_t  // First half of fall
        } else {
            2.0 * (1.0 - local_t) * (1.0 - local_t)  // Second half of fall
        }
    }
}
```

### Per-Character Phase Variation:
```rust
fn char_phase_offset(&self, char_idx: usize) -> f64 {
    // Deterministic pseudo-random based on seed and index
    let hash = self.seed.wrapping_mul(char_idx as u64 + 1);
    (hash % 1000) as f64 / 1000.0 * self.phase_variation
}

// In brightness calculation:
let t = (time * speed + char_phase_offset(idx)).rem_euclid(1.0);
let brightness = min_brightness + (1.0 - min_brightness) * breathing_curve(t, asymmetry);
```

This creates a subtle wave effect across the text as characters breathe slightly out of sync.

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_organic_asymmetric_rise_fast: Rise duration < fall duration
- test_organic_symmetric_equal: asymmetry=0 has equal rise/fall
- test_organic_phase_variation_spreads: Adjacent chars different phase
- test_organic_seed_deterministic: Same seed = same pattern
- test_organic_min_brightness_floor: Never below min
- test_organic_max_brightness_ceiling: Peaks at 1.0
- test_organic_cycle_complete: Returns to start after 1/speed seconds

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot organic pulse at t=0, 0.1, 0.3, 0.5 (shows asymmetry)
- Side-by-side: regular Pulse vs OrganicPulse

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Organic Breathing Pulse
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-3bix","title":"Text Effects: Wave/Position Animation System","description":"## Background
Most terminal UIs render static text. Adding per-character positional offsets creates dynamic, living text that's a major differentiator for FrankenTUI.

## Implementation

Add Direction enum for cascade/wave direction:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default)]
pub enum Direction {
    #[default]
    Down,       // Characters fall from above
    Up,         // Characters rise from below
    Left,       // Characters slide from right
    Right,      // Characters slide from left
}
```

Add CharacterOffset (internal, not exposed):
```rust
struct CharacterOffset { 
    dx: i16,  // Horizontal offset in cells
    dy: i16,  // Vertical offset in rows
}
```

Add TextEffect variants:
```rust
TextEffect::Wave {
    amplitude: f64,   // Max offset in cells (typically 1-3)
    wavelength: f64,  // Characters per wave cycle (typically 5-15)
    speed: f64,       // Waves per second
    direction: Direction,  // Wave travel direction
}

TextEffect::Bounce {
    height: f64,      // Max bounce height in cells
    speed: f64,       // Bounces per second
    stagger: f64,     // Delay between adjacent chars (0-1)
    damping: f64,     // How quickly bounce settles (0.8-0.99)
}

TextEffect::Shake {
    intensity: f64,   // Max offset (typically 0.5-2)
    speed: f64,       // Shakes per second
    seed: u64,        // For deterministic randomness
}

TextEffect::Cascade {
    speed: f64,       // Characters per second
    direction: Direction,
    stagger: f64,     // Delay between chars (0-1 of total duration)
}
```

### Offset Calculation:
```rust
impl StyledText {
    fn char_offset(&self, idx: usize, total: usize, time: f64) -> CharacterOffset {
        // Sum offsets from all position-modifying effects
        let mut offset = CharacterOffset { dx: 0, dy: 0 };
        for effect in &self.effects {
            match effect {
                TextEffect::Wave { amplitude, wavelength, speed, direction } => {
                    let phase = (idx as f64 / wavelength + time * speed) * TAU;
                    let wave = (phase.sin() * amplitude) as i16;
                    match direction {
                        Direction::Up | Direction::Down => offset.dy += wave,
                        Direction::Left | Direction::Right => offset.dx += wave,
                    }
                }
                // ... other effects
            }
        }
        offset
    }
}
```

### Terminal Bounds Handling:
- Use saturating arithmetic for all position calculations
- If y + dy < 0, clamp to 0 (don't wrap)
- If x + dx >= width, clamp to width-1

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_wave_offset_sinusoidal: Offset follows sine curve
- test_wave_amplitude_respected: Max offset equals amplitude
- test_wave_wavelength_period: Characters wavelength apart have same offset
- test_wave_direction_up_down: Vertical wave affects dy only
- test_wave_direction_left_right: Horizontal wave affects dx only
- test_bounce_starts_high: Initial position offset by height
- test_bounce_settles: Offset approaches 0 over time with damping
- test_bounce_stagger: Adjacent chars offset in time
- test_shake_bounded: Offset never exceeds intensity
- test_shake_deterministic: Same seed + time = same offset
- test_cascade_reveals_in_order: Characters appear in direction order
- test_offset_bounds_saturate: No overflow at terminal edges
- test_negative_offset_clamped: dy=-5 at row 0 doesn't wrap

## Visual Tests  
- Snapshot Wave at t=0, t=0.25, t=0.5, t=0.75 (shows motion)
- Snapshot Bounce settling sequence
- Snapshot Cascade reveal progression

## Performance
Offset calculation: <50ns per character

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Wave/Position Animation System
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
